title: Implement _TDummyDocumentReference delegating to the in-memory store
blocks: Query filter/sort + exports tasks — they build on a fully-working dummy api surface.

status_instructions.on_followup: If a downstream consumer needs additional delegated members, append to the final task's `side_context`.

description: Create a library-private `_TDummyDocumentReference<T>` that `implements DocumentReference<T>` and wraps a reference to the owning `TDummyFirestoreApi<T>` plus a concrete id. `.id`, `.path`, `.get()`, `.update(...)`, `.delete()`, `.snapshots()`, and any other member used inside the monorepo delegate back to the dummy api's in-memory store. Unsupported members (e.g. `.collection(subPath)`, `.parent`) throw `UnimplementedError` with a dummy-mode-guidance message. Replace the minimal placeholder used in task 09's createDoc response with this full class, and also wire `getDocRefById` / `getDocRefByIdWithConverter` overrides to return an instance bound to the requested id.
context_for_junior: `createDoc` on the real api returns a `DocumentReference<T>`, and some services immediately call `.update(...)` or `.get()` on it. If our dummy returns a placeholder that throws on `.update(...)`, those services break. So we build a proper stub that forwards `.update(...)` into the dummy api's `updateDoc`, `.get()` into the dummy api's `getByIdWithConverter`, `.snapshots()` into the dummy api's `streamDocByIdWithConverter`, and `.delete()` into the dummy api's `deleteDoc`. Members we do not use in the monorepo (like subcollection navigation) stay unimplemented — we will add them only if and when a real caller needs them.
why_it_matters: This is the last piece of the 'drop-in replacement' promise. With it, every existing service works unchanged in dummy mode.
instructions: Create `_TDummyDocumentReference<T> implements DocumentReference<T>` as a library-private class colocated with the dummy api file (part file if you chose that route earlier).
`get id` returns `_id`. `get path` returns `'${_api._pathSnapshot}/$_id'` (expose `_pathSnapshot` via a package-private getter if needed).

`get()` calls `_api.getByIdWithConverter(id: _id)` and converts the TurboResponse to the DocumentSnapshot shape the real DocumentReference returns. Since building a full DocumentSnapshot is elaborate, return a minimal stub `DocumentSnapshot<T>` — create another library-private class `_TDummyDocumentSnapshot<T> implements DocumentSnapshot<T>` with `data()`, `exists`, `id`, `reference` delegating to the dummy api.

`update(Map<Object, Object?> data)` constructs a writeable-like merge against the stored map — or, more cleanly, delegates to a new package-private helper `_api.rawUpdateById(id: _id, data: data.cast<String, dynamic>())` that you add to the dummy api in this task. This helper must honour the latency + failure gate.

`snapshots()` returns a mapped stream from `_api.streamDocByIdWithConverter(id: _id)` that wraps each emitted `T?` in a `_TDummyDocumentSnapshot<T>` — including emitting a snapshot with `exists = false` when the underlying stream emits null.

Unsupported members (`collection`, `parent`, `withConverter`, any batch helpers) throw `UnimplementedError('dummy-mode: DocumentReference.<name> is not stubbed; if your feature needs it, extend _TDummyDocumentReference')`.
Replace every use of the placeholder from task 09 with this real stub.
Override `getDocRefById` / `getDocRefByIdWithConverter` on TDummyFirestoreApi<T> to return this stub directly (no store read — matches the real api's lazy-reference behaviour).
Run `make analyze package=turbo_firestore_api` and the new unit tests.
goals: createDoc returns a DocumentReference that supports the monorepo's real call sites (.id, .path, .get, .update, .delete, .snapshots).
getDocRefById / getDocRefByIdWithConverter return the same stub shape.
Unsupported members fail loudly with actionable messages.
non_goals: No caching of snapshots across calls.
No integration with the query filter/sort task.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: createDoc returns a DocumentReference whose `.id` and `.path` match the expected ids.
Unit test: the returned DocumentReference's `.get()` yields a DocumentSnapshot with the created entity's data.
Unit test: the returned DocumentReference's `.update(...)` mutates the store; a subsequent `.get()` reflects the update.
Unit test: `.delete()` removes the entity; `.get()` afterwards yields a snapshot with `exists = false`.
Unit test: `.snapshots()` emits the initial snapshot and re-emits null after delete.
Unit test: calling `.collection(...)` throws UnimplementedError with a dummy-mode message.
constraints: Library-private visibility for both stub classes.
Reuse existing helpers — do not duplicate materialisation or latency code.
`rawUpdateById` applies timestamps via TTimestampType to stay consistent with updateDoc.
tools: RepoPrompt MCP for searching all call sites of `DocumentReference` across the monorepo to confirm which members are actually consumed

testing.rules: No mocks. Use the synthetic DTO + writeable fixture from earlier tasks. Assert end-to-end: createDoc → DocumentReference → update/delete → store reflects → streams fan out.
scenarios: Given createDoc returns a DocumentReference, When `.id` is read, Then it matches the id passed or the deterministically generated id.
Given the returned DocumentReference, When `.get()` is awaited, Then the snapshot's data matches the stored entity.
Given the returned DocumentReference, When `.update({fieldA: newValue})` is awaited, Then a subsequent `.get()` reflects the update.
Given an active `.snapshots()` subscription, When `.delete()` is called, Then the subscriber receives a snapshot with `exists = false`.
Given the stub, When an unsupported member like `.collection('children')` is called, Then an UnimplementedError with a dummy-mode message is thrown.
testing.refactor_for_testability: If `DocumentSnapshot` stubbing becomes awkward, hoist its construction into a private helper taking only `id` + `data` + `exists`.

quality_bar.maintainability: Every member has a one-line dartdoc explaining the delegation path.
quality_bar.dry: All delegation flows through existing dummy api methods — no new mutation logic lives here.
quality_bar.single_responsibility: The stub does address + dispatch, nothing more.
quality_bar.performance: Every member is O(1) plus whatever the delegated method costs.
quality_bar.abuse_resistance: Unsupported members fail loudly with actionable messages rather than returning fake data.

side_context: Task 09 created a minimal _TDummyDocumentReference placeholder in lib/apis/t_dummy_firestore_api.dart. It implements DocumentReference<Map<String, dynamic>> with id, path, firestore working; everything else throws UnimplementedError. Expand this class with full dummy implementations as needed.

_TDummyDocumentReference constructor: const _TDummyDocumentReference({required String id, required String collectionPath, required FirebaseFirestore firestore}). Created by createDoc, updateDoc, createDocInBatch, updateDocInBatch, and deleteDocInBatch success paths.
ignore_for_file: subtype_of_sealed_class is already set at the top of the file.
Task 10 added _TDummyWriteBatch<T>, _TDummyTransaction<T>, and _TDummyDocumentSnapshot<T> to the same file. WriteBatch and Transaction now work end-to-end against the in-memory store.

createDoc/updateDoc/deleteDoc now handle writeBatch and transaction parameters: writeBatch delegates to *InBatch + _commitDummyBatch; transaction mutates inline and tracks touched ids for post-handler emission.
_TDummyDocumentSnapshot<T> is a minimal stub: id, reference, exists, data() work; metadata and get() throw UnimplementedError with dummy-mode message.
Test runner: /Users/codaveto/Flutter/bin/flutter test --no-pub works. 89 tests passing.
